picks.rationale: The Boston Celtics are playing at home in a decisive Game 7, where they historically perform well, holding a 27-10 all-time record in such games and a 6-2 record against the 76ers in Game 7s. Despite recent losses, Boston finished the regular season with a superior 56-26 record compared to Philadelphia's 45-37. Jayson Tatum is confirmed to play, and the Celtics' league-leading defense (107.2 PPG allowed) is expected to reassert itself on their home court.

Game 7s typically feature an increased number of possessions due to more aggressive play and intentional fouling in late-game situations, which tends to drive up the total score. The series has already seen an average of 210.5 points per game, with five of the six previous matchups exceeding the 206-point mark. The Celtics' offense is likely to push the pace after being held under 100 points in their last two outings, and the 76ers' defense ranked 19th in the regular season.

The Boston Celtics hold a significant advantage playing at home in Game 7, a scenario where they have a strong historical track record. They finished the regular season as the second seed in the Eastern Conference with a 56-26 record, showcasing their overall superiority. With star Jayson Tatum confirmed to be active and playing, the Celtics are well-positioned to secure the win and advance in the playoffs.

The Carolina Hurricanes enter this game with significant momentum, having swept their first-round series 4-0 against the Ottawa Senators and enjoying a week of rest. They also hold a dominant head-to-head record against the Flyers, winning 16 of their 20 all-time matchups and 8 of the last 10 encounters. Carolina's strong home-ice advantage and their status as the top seed in the East further solidify their position. Despite some injuries, the Hurricanes have demonstrated strong team play and offensive depth in the playoffs.

Historically, games between the Flyers and Hurricanes tend to be higher scoring, with the last 10 head-to-head matches averaging 5.8 goals and all 10 going Over 2.5 goals. All four regular-season matchups this year also extended beyond regulation, indicating competitive games where goals were exchanged. While both teams have shown strong defensive play in the playoffs, Carolina's offense has been potent, averaging 3.5 goals in their last 10 games, and the public betting trend shows movement towards the Over.

The Carolina Hurricanes are well-positioned to cover the -1.5 spread due to their superior recent form and significant rest advantage. They swept their previous series without ever trailing in a game, showcasing their dominance. The Flyers, coming off a grueling six-game series, may struggle to match Carolina's intensity and depth, especially on the road. Carolina's strong offensive output, averaging 3.5 goals in their last 10 games, combined with their suffocating defense, makes a multi-goal victory highly probable.

The New York Yankees are in excellent form, going 8-2 in their last 10 games, and have dominated the Orioles in recent head-to-head matchups, winning the last five games and covering the -1.5 spread in all of them. Their starting pitcher, Ryan Weathers, has a significantly better ERA and WHIP than Orioles' starter Kyle Bradish, who has struggled with walks. The betting market shows strong support for the Yankees, with their moneyline shortening and a massive 99% of the public backing them on the spread. This pick is further supported by the Yankees' strong run prevention compared to the Orioles' high runs allowed.

picks.game: San Francisco Giants @ Tampa Bay Rays
picks.rationale: The Tampa Bay Rays are in much better form than the San Francisco Giants, holding a 19-12 record and a strong 9-4 home record, while the Giants are struggling with a 13-19 record and a four-game losing streak. Although Yandy Díaz is day-to-day with an oblique injury, the Rays still have a strong lineup and pitching staff. The Giants also have several key players on the injured list, including Harrison Bader and Joel Peguero. The Rays' overall winning percentage of .613 significantly outpaces the Giants' .406, indicating a clear advantage for the home team.

The Chicago Cubs defeated the Arizona Diamondbacks 6-5 yesterday, extending the Diamondbacks' recent struggles to seven losses in their last ten games. The Cubs boast a strong 20-12 record, indicating consistent performance, while the Diamondbacks are just 16-15 and have a 4-6 record over their last ten outings. This recent head-to-head victory and the Diamondbacks' poor form create a favorable situation for the Cubs at home. The Cubs have demonstrated the ability to win close games, which is crucial against a Diamondbacks team that can score runs.

picks.game: New York Mets @ Los Angeles Angels
picks.rationale: The New York Mets have a historical edge over the Los Angeles Angels, holding a 16-14 all-time record and currently riding a three-game winning streak against them, with their last win being 6-3 on July 23, 2025. The Angels are in a significant slump, currently on a five-game losing streak. While both teams have struggled this season, the Mets have shown better recent form against the Angels (3-2 in last 5 H2H). This combination of historical dominance and the Angels' current poor performance makes the Mets a confident moneyline pick.

The Milwaukee Brewers, with a 17-14 record, are performing better overall than the Washington Nationals, who stand at 15-18. While the Nationals have shown some recent competitiveness with a 5-5 record in their last 10 games, the Brewers have a stronger season-long performance. There are no significant injury concerns reported for either team that would drastically alter the matchup. The Brewers' slightly better record and overall team strength provide a solid foundation for a moneyline pick against a Nationals team that, despite some individual hot streaks like CJ Abrams', remains below .500.

picks.game: Seattle Sounders FC @ Sporting Kansas City
picks.rationale: Seattle Sounders are in excellent form with a 6-1-1 record and have not lost a league game since February 28th. They boast a strong defense, having gone 403 consecutive minutes without conceding a road goal. Conversely, Sporting Kansas City is on a six-game losing streak, has scored the fewest goals in the league, and has conceded 11 goals in their last three matches. Sporting KC is also significantly hampered by injuries, with key defenders Kwaku Agyabeng, Diego Borges, and Justin Reynolds all ruled out, further weakening their already struggling defense.

The San Jose Earthquakes are in phenomenal form, holding a 9-0-1 record and winning six consecutive games, including nine of their last ten. Their offense is potent, averaging 2.5 goals per game. Toronto FC has a mediocre 3-4-3 record and recently suffered a home loss. Despite some historical draws in their head-to-head matchups, San Jose's current dominant form and high-scoring output provide a strong edge at favorable plus-money odds.

Nashville SC is enjoying an outstanding season with a 7-1-1 record, placing them at the top of the Eastern Conference. In stark contrast, the Philadelphia Union are struggling significantly with a 1-2-7 record, sitting at the bottom of the conference. While the head-to-head history is evenly split, Nashville's vastly superior current form and league standing present exceptional value at +240 odds, making them a high-confidence underdog pick.

Chicago Fire FC is in strong form, particularly when playing at home, having secured four home victories and currently holding 3rd position in the league. Conversely, FC Cincinnati has a very poor away record this season, failing to win any away games. Although both teams face some injury concerns, Chicago's consistent home performance combined with Cincinnati's struggles on the road makes the Fire a confident moneyline pick.

The head-to-head history between Real Salt Lake and Portland Timbers suggests a high-scoring affair, with 61.36% of their 44 previous meetings going over 2.5 goals and an average of 3.05 goals per match. Real Salt Lake averages 1.9 goals per game and has a perfect 15-0-0 record when scoring more than two goals. Portland Timbers average 1.4 goals per game while conceding 2.1 goals per game. Furthermore, RSL is dealing with multiple defensive and midfield injuries, which could lead to a more open game and increased scoring opportunities for both sides.

Joel Alvarez, known as 'El Fenomeno,' enters this bout with an impressive 23-3-0 professional MMA record and is an active fighter on the UFC roster. He boasts a high finishing rate, securing 17 victories by submission, and has been in excellent form, winning eight of his last nine fights. Given the lack of readily available fight history or recent form for his opponent, Bryce Logan, Alvarez's proven track record of dominance and finishing ability makes him a very strong favorite. His consistent performance against various opponents provides a clear edge in this matchup.

Karl Williams, a UFC heavyweight, boasts a solid 10-2-0 professional record and has a proven grappling-heavy style. Despite a unanimous decision loss in his last fight in August 2024, he secured three consecutive unanimous decision victories in the UFC prior to that. With no available professional fight information or recent form for Maxwell Djantou Nana, Williams's established UFC experience, effective wrestling, and overall winning record position him as a strong favorite. His ability to control the pace and take opponents down should lead to a victory.

Humberto Bandenay holds a professional MMA record of 21-8-0 and is currently riding a three-fight win streak, with his most recent victory recorded in July 2022. Although he had a brief stint in the UFC, his overall career demonstrates a strong finishing ability, with 7 knockouts and 8 submissions. Sang Won Kim (14-6-1) lost his last Road to UFC fight in August 2023. Bandenay's more extensive and successful overall record across various promotions, including PFL, gives him a significant advantage as the favorite in this matchup.
